title: Devillea pyrenaea

intro: Devillea pyrenaea, also known as Pyrenean devil's-bit scabious, is a perennial plant species belonging to the genus Devillea within the family Dipsacaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Devillea pyrenaea include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the latter being a popular herbal preparation made from the plant's aerial parts.
